title: Randia angatensis
intro: Randia angatensis is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a flowering plant. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, to treat various health issues due to its reported health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory tract infections. The bioactive constituents of Randia angatensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Randia angatensis is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and may also be formulated into capsules or tablets for convenient consumption.
taxonomy: Randia angatensis belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Randia angatensis being a part of the tribe Palicoureeae. The genus Randia itself is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical regions, particularly in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. The species Randia angatensis is a type of vine that is found in the Philippines. Its classification within the plant kingdom is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Gentianales, family Rubiaceae, genus Randia, species R. angatensis.
